title: Race-group cross-fitted hierarchical categorical priors plus CatBoost ordered boosting
summary: CatBoost’s ordered boosting and ordered categorical statistics were designed specifically to reduce prediction shift from target leakage.
rationale: CatBoost’s ordered boosting and ordered categorical statistics were designed specifically to reduce prediction shift from target leakage. This dataset has very high-cardinality Driver and Driver-Race structure, including sparse and unseen identities, so hierarchical OOF priors are likely higher-upside than adding more manual thresholds.
implementation_hint: Train a CatBoost classifier with raw categorical columns and compare it to the current numeric-engineered tree baseline. Add fold-safe hierarchical encodings computed only from training folds: Driver, Driver×Compound, Driver×Race, Race×Compound, Year×Race×Stint, with empirical-Bayes shrinkage toward broader parents. Use race-year grouped folds so encodings never see the held-out event history.
expected_effect: Better generalization on sparse drivers/races and less overfit than naive frequency or mean encodings.
risk: Random-row CV can make this look better than it is; evaluate under LeaveOneGroupOut or grouped folds by Race_Year/Year to confirm the gain survives leakage-safe splits.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
